paper_title: Expressed Sequence Tag Analysis and Core Analyses

methods.method_name: Full-Length Clone Identification and Function Assignment
methods.description: This method identifies putative full-length sequences for function assignment by grouping EST sequences into alignment classes based on BLASTX results, and assigns protein coding frames and functions.

steps.description: The EST sequences were grouped into nine alignment classes based on the subject coverage (CovS) and identities of the BLASTX results.
steps.searchable_summary: group EST sequences into alignment classes using BLASTX results

steps.description: For sequences in F-1, F-2, M-1, and M-2 alignment classes, the protein coding frame and putative function were assigned based on the BLASTX description.
steps.searchable_summary: assign protein coding frame and function using BLASTX description

steps.description: Sequences were aligned using CLUSTAL W with their corresponding 10 most similar GenBank protein database entries obtained from the BLASTX results.
steps.searchable_summary: align sequences using CLUSTAL W with GenBank entries

steps.description: RPS BLAST was used to identify the COG to which each sequence in the non-redundant collection could be assigned.
A motif search was made among all non-redundant sequences using RPS BLAST against the Pfam database.
steps.searchable_summary: perform motif search using RPS BLAST against Pfam

steps.description: Codon usage tables were created for each organism using the subsets of non-redundant ESTs falling in the alignment classes F-1, F-2, M-1, and M-2.
steps.searchable_summary: create codon usage tables using non-redundant ESTs

methods.method_name: Raw Data Processing and Core Analyses
methods.description: This method involves the processing of raw EST data, including quality checking, vector clipping, clustering, and annotation to create a non-redundant set of sequences.

steps.description: Sequences were subjected to quality checking and vector clipping using the Trimest, Trimseq, and Vectorstrip programs of EMBOSS.
steps.searchable_summary: perform quality checking and vector clipping using EMBOSS tools

steps.description: Sequences longer than 50 nt were subjected to clustering using the Contig Assembling Program (CAP3) to detect sequence redundancy.
steps.searchable_summary: cluster sequences using CAP3 to detect redundancy

steps.description: The longest sequence from each assembly was selected and pooled with the singletons to form the non-redundant set.
steps.searchable_summary: select longest sequence from each assembly and pool with singletons

steps.description: Non-redundant sequences were subjected to automated annotation, including searches against the NCBI non-redundant protein database using BLASTX.
steps.searchable_summary: annotate sequences using BLASTX against NCBI database
